steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The logical relationship is cause-effect. Sentence 1 states that these methods process shapes numbering in the thousands (a lighter load). The blank needs a transition signaling that their economic advantage over surface modeling follows from that lighter load.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Instead,’: signals contrast or substitution, implying sentence 2 replaces or contradicts sentence 1. The two sentences are complementary, not contrasting. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘For example,’: signals exemplification. Being economical would have to be a specific instance of a general claim in sentence 1, but sentence 1 makes no such general claim. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Specifically,’: signals narrowing to a specific case of a broader point. Sentence 2 adds a new comparative fact rather than narrowing sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘As such,’: signals that the conclusion in sentence 2 follows from the situation described in sentence 1 (lighter processing load → more economical), so fits. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
